seoTitle: Penfield Gardens vs Pennington — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Penfield Gardens and Pennington. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Penfield Gardens vs Pennington: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Penfield Gardens and Pennington.
heading: Penfield Gardens vs Pennington — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Penfield Gardens, South Australia and Pennington, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Penfield Gardens has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to Pennington (39/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Penfield Gardens or Pennington safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Penfield Gardens has a flood risk score of 50/100, while Pennington scores 49/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Penfield Gardens scores 54/100 for bushfire risk. Pennington scores 33/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Penfield Gardens or Pennington?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Penfield Gardens has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.35% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
